title: Alsine mantica
uses: Alsine mantica, also known as Alsine wulfeniastrum, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and flowers have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, it is sometimes used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. However, its efficacy and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically proven. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Alsine mantica.
taxonomy: Alsine mantica is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae. Alsine mantica belongs to the genus Alsine, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as spring whitlowgrasses. The species Alsine mantica is native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. It is often found growing in damp to wet areas, such as meadows and stream banks. Alsine mantica is also known by its synonym Montia fontana.
